surface.name: Notion API quickstart with a personal access token
surface.selection_basis: Notion's current Get started overview points to the API quickstart, which explicitly creates a private workspace page with a personal access token and one request. It recommends this personal-token route for the fastest user-owned script or CLI start and avoids the internal-connection sharing and public OAuth paths.

alternatives_considered.name: Internal connection developer quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The current API quickstart says a personal access token is faster for a user-owned script or CLI, while internal connections demonstrate team-owned automation and explicit page sharing.

alternatives_considered.name: Notion CLI and Workers quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a separate Workers product route that installs the CLI, deploys a worker, and runs a tool rather than making the API quickstart's one page-creation request.

alternatives_considered.name: Public connection with OAuth
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: OAuth is the multi-workspace route, while the selected API quickstart uses a personal token for the developer's own workspace.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Notion account and workspace, generate a personal access token, send the API quickstart page-creation request, and open the resulting private page.
assumed_prior_state: No Notion account, workspace, workspace role, developer-portal access, personal access token, environment variable, private API-created page, or prior request is assumed.
A browser and terminal with cURL are available; an email, Google account, or Microsoft account is available for the current signup choices.
No account was created, provider was authorized, terms were accepted, workspace was created or joined, administrator setting was changed, token was created, or API request was sent during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Create a page in the developer's Notion workspace with a personal access token and a single API request.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The intended terminal is a private page named `Hello from the API`, created from the published Markdown request and visible through the returned URL or the Private section of the Notion sidebar.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The API returns a page object with an ID and `url`; opening it shows the heading, paragraph, and bullet list, and the guide states that the first request has been made.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The one-request page terminal is explicit, but the required signup, workspace, role, and developer-portal transition is hidden behind the current client-rendered Notion application.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The API quickstart stops after checking the created private page and then labels later APIs as next steps. A new developer cannot reproduce its personal-token prerequisite because the public signup shows only initial identity choices and the developer portal requires client-side JavaScript.

prerequisites.requirement: Have a Notion account and workspace.
Have permission to create a personal access token; Business and Enterprise workspaces restrict PAT creation by default and require a workspace owner to enable it.
Create, copy, and securely store a personal access token because it cannot be viewed again.
Have a terminal and cURL for the selected one-request route.

primary_path.action: Open the Notion developer Get started overview and select the API quickstart.
primary_path.output: The personal-token API quickstart is presented alongside internal and public connection routes.
primary_path.success_signal: The API quickstart link is visible as the hands-on first-request route.

primary_path.action: Open the API quickstart and review its Notion account prerequisite, personal-token step, page request, and check-result terminal.
primary_path.output: The guide requires a Notion account before opening Personal access tokens in the Developer portal.
primary_path.success_signal: The account prerequisite and one-request terminal are explicit.

primary_path.action: Open Notion signup from a no-account state.
details: The public signup exposes work email, Google, and Microsoft choices.
Continuing acknowledges the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy.
primary_path.output: Only the initial identity choices are publicly visible; the application requires JavaScript for continuation.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer can identify the initial signup methods.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Research stops because provider authorization or email verification, account completion, workspace creation or join, role assignment, and return to the Developer portal are not publicly enumerable.

candidate_path_gap.reason: The public signup exposes email, Google, Microsoft, and legal acknowledgement, but not the selected method's verification, account completion, workspace onboarding, role outcome, or Developer portal convergence.
candidate_path_gap.impact: The personal access token cannot be reached reproducibly from no account, so the otherwise complete one-request API quickstart cannot form an end-to-end public candidate.

branches.path: Enter the email and continue into Notion's client-rendered verification and workspace onboarding.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Can lead to workspace creation or an organization-controlled workspace outcome, but the intermediate sequence is not public.

branches.path: Choose the provider and complete its external authorization before returning to Notion onboarding.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces email verification but leaves provider grants and workspace convergence undocumented.

branches.condition: The account and permitted workspace already exist
branches.path: Open Personal access tokens, click New token, name and create it, select a workspace if prompted, copy it, export `NOTION_API_KEY`, send the published `POST /v1/pages` request with API version `2026-03-11`, and open the returned page URL.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Reaches the documented private-page terminal after the blocked account transition.

branches.condition: PAT creation is disabled on a Business or Enterprise workspace
branches.path: Ask a workspace owner to enable personal access tokens under Settings & members > Connections, then return to token creation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an administrator permission and wait before the API credential exists.

friction_gates.description: The API quickstart requires an existing Notion account and workspace.
The current signup offers work email, Google, or Microsoft.
Continuing signup acknowledges Notion's Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy.
Business and Enterprise workspaces restrict personal access token creation by default and may require owner action.
The personal access token is shown once and must be stored securely for the request.

time_to_first_success.value: Under two minutes for the API quickstart when the developer already has a Notion account and terminal.

sources.title: API quickstart - Notion Docs
sections_used: Step 1: Get a personal access token
evidence_supported: Exact API version and page request

sources.title: Notion
evidence_supported: Developer portal requires client-side JavaScript and exposes no reproducible account or token-creation state publicly

uncertainties.question: What exact current signup and workspace sequence reaches a role that can create a personal access token in the Notion Developer portal?
uncertainties.impact: Without that convergence, the API credential and page-creation request cannot be reached from no account.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The signup page exposes only initial identity choices and legal acknowledgement, while the Developer portal itself is client-rendered and the quickstart assumes the account already exists.

excluded_after_success: Creating databases, querying content, page sharing, internal connections, OAuth, public distribution, comments, files, Workers, webhooks, rate-limit handling, and production token management occur after the first private page is verified.
